themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their advocacy for 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to public services and benefits. This theme is prominently featured in Spain (VOX), Italy (Lega/Fratelli d'Italia), and France (Rassemblement National), reflecting a shared transnational strategy to reshape welfare and housing policies along nationalist lines.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: VOX's 'national priority' motion in Valencia and agreements with PP in Aragón and Extremadura prioritize Spaniards in public aid, housing, and social services.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega's policies in Italy, such as coastal concessions and housing reforms, reflect nationalist priorities, while Fratelli d'Italia supports similar measures in regional governments.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN's Jordan Bardella advocates for replacing EU flags with French symbols and prioritizing French citizens in economic policies, aligning with 'national priority' rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS's policies, such as opposition to EU migrant quotas, align with nationalist priorities, though the party has not explicitly adopted 'national priority' language.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump's policies and rhetoric continue to shape right-wing discourse globally, with extensive coverage of his administration's actions, including military engagements, immigration reforms, and economic measures. This theme resonates strongly in the USA, UK (Reform UK), Spain (VOX), and Italy (Fratelli d'Italia/Lega), where Trump's influence is either directly cited or mirrored in local policies.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's policies, such as the Iran war, immigration reforms, and economic measures, dominate domestic right-wing discourse and are frequently referenced in global coverage.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK's immigration policies and rhetoric closely mirror Trump's 'America First' approach, with Nigel Farage often invoking Trump's strategies.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: VOX's anti-immigration stance and calls for stricter border controls reflect Trump's influence, particularly in their 'national priority' policies.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties, including Lega and Fratelli d'Italia, frequently reference Trump's policies, particularly on immigration and national sovereignty.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN's Jordan Bardella and Marine Le Pen often cite Trump's policies as inspiration for their own nationalist and anti-immigration agendas.

themes.title: Hungary’s political shift under new leadership
themes.blurb: Hungary's recent election has ushered in a new political era under Péter Magyar and the Tisza Party, marking a significant shift away from Viktor Orbán's long-standing rule. This transition is closely watched in Poland (PiS), Germany (AfD), and the USA (MAGA), where right-wing parties are assessing the implications for EU relations, national sovereignty, and transnational conservative alliances.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Péter Magyar's Tisza Party has taken power, ending Orbán's 16-year rule and promising reforms to unlock frozen EU funds and restore democratic norms.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS closely monitors Hungary's political shift, particularly its implications for EU relations and national sovereignty, as Poland faces similar challenges.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD views Hungary's political transition as a potential model for challenging EU policies and asserting national sovereignty in Germany.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump and his allies see Hungary's shift as an opportunity to realign conservative alliances in Europe and challenge liberal EU policies.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric and policies are increasingly linked to incidents of political violence, both in Europe and the USA. This theme is evident in Italy (Fratelli d'Italia/Lega), Spain (VOX), the UK (Reform UK), and the USA (MAGA), where inflammatory language and actions have escalated tensions and led to real-world confrontations.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega and Fratelli d'Italia's rhetoric, particularly on immigration and national identity, has been linked to rising political violence and protests.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: VOX's anti-immigration and nationalist rhetoric has sparked counter-protests and vandalism, particularly in regions where the party holds power.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK's inflammatory language on immigration and national identity has led to protests and clashes, particularly during local election campaigns.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's rhetoric has been linked to multiple incidents of political violence, including the recent White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting and threats against political opponents.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a surge in popularity, as reflected in recent polls. This trend is evident in Germany (AfD), France (Rassemblement National), Sweden (Sverigedemokraterna), the UK (Reform UK), and Norway (Fremskrittspartiet), where these parties are gaining ground and reshaping the political landscape.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads in Berlin polls and gains traction in regional elections, reflecting a broader surge in far-right support across Germany.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN leads in French polls, with Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella positioning the party as the frontrunner for the 2027 presidential election.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Sverigedemokraterna's support remains strong, with the party influencing Sweden's immigration and citizenship policies.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK is gaining ground in local elections, particularly in areas where the Conservative Party is losing support.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP leads in Norwegian polls, with Sylvi Listhaug positioning herself as a potential prime ministerial candidate.
